sets.methodology: BuildKit Draft Score = 0.75 * z(shrink(GIH WR, k=200)) + 0.25 * z(shrink(IWD, k=200)), computed from 17lands' public PremierDraft card_ratings (CC BY 4.0). shrink(x, n, k=200) = (n*x + k*mean(x over the pool)) / (n + k) is empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the pool mean weighted by n = GIH game_count, so two cards with the SAME raw win rate but different sample sizes never tie: the low-sample card regresses harder toward the mean and cannot outrank the high-sample card. z(y) = (y - mean(y)) / population_stdev(y) is computed across EVERY card in the pool regardless of rarity -- rarity-neutral, no separate curve for commons vs mythics. Grade bands on the final score: S>=+1.25, A>=+0.5, B>=-0.25, C>=-1.0, D>=-2.0, else F; a card with no GIH data at all is 'unrated'. GIH WR = ever_drawn_win_rate, IWD = drawn_improvement_win_rate, ALSA = avg_seen, ATA = avg_pick, OH WR = opening_hand_win_rate -- 17lands' own field names, carried through unchanged. Per-color-pair rows use the identical formula computed over just that pair's card pool; a pair only publishes when 17lands' response for it is verifiably distinct from the set's overall response (seventeenlands.pair_data_is_distinct) -- 17lands' public no-auth endpoint does not currently filter by color pair for every set/pair combination, and a non-distinct pair is omitted rather than republished as fake per-pair signal. EARLY DATA -- upgrading as samples grow (150,696 total games so far). 17lands' color-pair filter returned no data distinct from the overall set this run, so pair_rows is empty rather than a duplicate of overall_rows.

sets.sealed_methodology: BuildKit Draft Score = 0.75 * z(shrink(GIH WR, k=200)) + 0.25 * z(shrink(IWD, k=200)), computed from 17lands' public PremierDraft card_ratings (CC BY 4.0). shrink(x, n, k=200) = (n*x + k*mean(x over the pool)) / (n + k) is empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the pool mean weighted by n = GIH game_count, so two cards with the SAME raw win rate but different sample sizes never tie: the low-sample card regresses harder toward the mean and cannot outrank the high-sample card. z(y) = (y - mean(y)) / population_stdev(y) is computed across EVERY card in the pool regardless of rarity -- rarity-neutral, no separate curve for commons vs mythics. Grade bands on the final score: S>=+1.25, A>=+0.5, B>=-0.25, C>=-1.0, D>=-2.0, else F; a card with no GIH data at all is 'unrated'. GIH WR = ever_drawn_win_rate, IWD = drawn_improvement_win_rate, ALSA = avg_seen, ATA = avg_pick, OH WR = opening_hand_win_rate -- 17lands' own field names, carried through unchanged. Per-color-pair rows use the identical formula computed over just that pair's card pool; a pair only publishes when 17lands' response for it is verifiably distinct from the set's overall response (seventeenlands.pair_data_is_distinct) -- 17lands' public no-auth endpoint does not currently filter by color pair for every set/pair combination, and a non-distinct pair is omitted rather than republished as fake per-pair signal. Sealed rows use the IDENTICAL BuildKit Draft Score formula computed fresh over the Sealed card pool's own mean/stdev (a card's sealed grade is never mixed with its draft grade) -- only the sample-confidence bins differ (Sealed: (20, 80, 300), vs draft's (100, 400, 1500)) since Sealed's per-card game counts run structurally lower. Sourced from 17lands' public card_ratings endpoint with `format=Sealed` (CC BY 4.0). 17lands' public Sealed card_ratings endpoint returned 8,506 ever-drawn games but NO per-card win-rate stats this run (verified live 2026-07-11: win rates appear unavailable for Sealed via this public no-auth route even when game-count totals are non-zero) -- sealed_rows ships honest-absent rather than a page of every card graded 'unrated'.

archetypes.methodology: Set-level archetype (color) win rates from 17lands' public color_ratings endpoint (CC BY 4.0) -- PremierDraft, splashes combined. win_rate = wins/games for each real color/color-pair row; a row with zero games renders win_rate 'null' and carries no rank rather than a divide-by-zero guess. 17lands' own `is_summary` rows (aggregate totals across every archetype) are skipped entirely -- never a real archetype. rank = the archetype's win_rate position WITHIN its own color_count group -- mono colors are ranked only against other mono colors, two-color pairs only against other two-color pairs, and any 3+ color combination 17lands actually reports only against other combinations of the same length -- so a mono archetype never outranks a pair just because it carries a higher raw win rate; 1 = best in its group. Confidence bins on sample_size=games: >= 1,000 high, >= 400 medium, else low (every real row has sample_size >= 0, so there is no separate 'sample' floor here). Only the color/pair combinations 17lands' endpoint actually returns this run are published -- unreported color combinations are never synthesized.

sets.methodology: BuildKit Draft Score = 0.75 * z(shrink(GIH WR, k=200)) + 0.25 * z(shrink(IWD, k=200)), computed from 17lands' public PremierDraft card_ratings (CC BY 4.0). shrink(x, n, k=200) = (n*x + k*mean(x over the pool)) / (n + k) is empirical-Bayes shrinkage toward the pool mean weighted by n = GIH game_count, so two cards with the SAME raw win rate but different sample sizes never tie: the low-sample card regresses harder toward the mean and cannot outrank the high-sample card. z(y) = (y - mean(y)) / population_stdev(y) is computed across EVERY card in the pool regardless of rarity -- rarity-neutral, no separate curve for commons vs mythics. Grade bands on the final score: S>=+1.25, A>=+0.5, B>=-0.25, C>=-1.0, D>=-2.0, else F; a card with no GIH data at all is 'unrated'. GIH WR = ever_drawn_win_rate, IWD = drawn_improvement_win_rate, ALSA = avg_seen, ATA = avg_pick, OH WR = opening_hand_win_rate -- 17lands' own field names, carried through unchanged. Per-color-pair rows use the identical formula computed over just that pair's card pool; a pair only publishes when 17lands' response for it is verifiably distinct from the set's overall response (seventeenlands.pair_data_is_distinct) -- 17lands' public no-auth endpoint does not currently filter by color pair for every set/pair combination, and a non-distinct pair is omitted rather than republished as fake per-pair signal. This set's 17lands card_ratings response totalled 97 ever-drawn games this run -- below the 5,000-game publish floor, so no rows are graded.
attribution: Win rates via 17lands.com public card ratings (CC BY 4.0)

archetypes.methodology: Set-level archetype (color) win rates from 17lands' public color_ratings endpoint (CC BY 4.0) -- PremierDraft, splashes combined. win_rate = wins/games for each real color/color-pair row; a row with zero games renders win_rate 'null' and carries no rank rather than a divide-by-zero guess. 17lands' own `is_summary` rows (aggregate totals across every archetype) are skipped entirely -- never a real archetype. rank = the archetype's win_rate position WITHIN its own color_count group -- mono colors are ranked only against other mono colors, two-color pairs only against other two-color pairs, and any 3+ color combination 17lands actually reports only against other combinations of the same length -- so a mono archetype never outranks a pair just because it carries a higher raw win rate; 1 = best in its group. Confidence bins on sample_size=games: >= 1,000 high, >= 400 medium, else low (every real row has sample_size >= 0, so there is no separate 'sample' floor here). Only the color/pair combinations 17lands' endpoint actually returns this run are published -- unreported color combinations are never synthesized.

cube.methodology: IMPORTANT: these are DAY-1 PRIORS, NOT real Planar Cube win rates -- Planar Cube Draft launched on MTG Arena 2026-07-21 and 17lands has not yet accumulated per-card telemetry for it (verified live 2026-07-21: their public 'Cube - Planar' card_ratings endpoint returns HTTP 200 with zero rows). EVERY card in the pool (the constant 200-card planar core + the current week's 360-card rotating planar module, per Wizards' own announcement article) gets a real tier -- we never withhold a grade behind an 'unrated' placeholder. Each card is graded by the BEST available signal, tried in this order, with the basis always shown on the row: (1) REAL live 'Cube - Planar' data, the instant 17lands starts publishing a real win rate for that card -- this module upgrades automatically, since the live fetch is attempted every refresh; (2) a 'Cube - Powered' prior (the prior Arena Powered Cube event, June 2-23 2026) when 17lands' live endpoint carries a real win rate for that exact card; (3) a cross-set prior -- that card's rating in ANY of the recent premier-draft sets we check (not limited to the Draft Ranker's own 3, we look back further specifically for this), newest set wins ties; (4) a HEURISTIC prior when none of the above have data -- a transparent, documented (NOT statistical) score from the card's own Scryfall-grounded rarity + mana value + type line (see CUBE_HEURISTIC_* constants for the exact weights), clearly labeled 'heuristic' and never presented as a win rate or a real performance signal. BuildKit Draft Score (the identical empirical-Bayes-shrunk z-score formula as the Draft Ranker -- see DRAFT_SCORE_METHODOLOGY) is computed over ONLY the cube pool's own mean/stdev of whatever REAL priors ((1)/(2)/(3)) were found this run -- a prior card's score is never benchmarked against its original set's baseline, and heuristic rows never get a draft_score (they carry heuristic_score instead, on its own separate never-comparable scale). Confidence on any (2)/(3) prior row is capped at 'low' no matter how large the borrowed sample was -- a card's performance in a DIFFERENT format/cube/set is real data but not the same signal as its performance in Planar Cube, so it can never earn 'medium'/'high' from a borrowed prior. Heuristic (4) rows always carry 'sample' confidence -- the lowest tier, rendered faded -- since no performance data backs them at all. Only a real (1) live-data row can reach 'medium'/'high' confidence. If very few cards found a real prior this run, the z-score itself can be mathematically undefined (a population of one has no variance) -- those rows fall back to a plain win-rate-floor grade (S>=56%, A>=53%, B>=50.5%, C>=48%, else D -- the same floors as the Limited tier list) so a real recorded win rate is never hidden behind an 'unrated' placeholder either. Uncertainty is communicated ONLY through confidence + fading + the visible basis label -- never by omitting a tier.
